existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: ACL Tear MRI Pattern

metadata.description: Characteristic MRI findings in acl tear.
C-shaped medial meniscus distributing load and resisting shear at the medial compartment.
Medial and lateral femoral condyles forming the proximal tibiofemoral articulation.
Fibrocartilaginous rim deepening the glenoid and anchoring capsulolabral structures.
Posterior cuff tendon providing external rotation strength.

metadata.claim_text: ACL tears are instability injuries diagnosed by history and exam, with reconstruction decisions shaped by goals and associated pathology.
Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for acl tear — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.
Lateral femoral condyle and posterior tibial plateau bone bruise pattern on MRI supports ACL injury mechanism.

metadata.description: Superior rotator cuff tendon initiating abduction and commonly torn in cuff pathology.

metadata.claim_text: Lachman test is more sensitive than anterior drawer for ACL insufficiency in the acute setting.

metadata.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
metadata.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after acl tear treatment

metadata.claim_text: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for acl tear require attending-defined progression gates.
Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating acl tear.

metadata.description: Lateral elbow stabilizer resisting varus stress.

metadata.claim_text: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for acl tear.

metadata.description: Common post-injury complication in acl tear.

metadata.claim_text: ACL Tear pathoanatomy centers on acl, medial-meniscus, lateral-meniscus and related stabilizers.

metadata.action: Prioritize prehab to regain extension and reduce arthrofibrosis risk before surgery

metadata.description: Return-to-play rehabilitation milestones for acl tear.
